media.description: The Pacers will be limited in resources in free agency if they don't shed more salary, but there are still depth pieces they can add for cheap.

items.title: Breaking: The Brooklyn Nets plan to sign Day’Ron Sharpe to a two-year deal worth roughly $20 million, sources told HoopsHype. Sharpe has impressed Brooklyn and is expected to have a bigger role this season. Agents Aaron Reilly and Reggie Berry of AMR Agency finalized the deal.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Breaking: The Brooklyn Nets plan to sign Day’Ron Sharpe to a two-year deal worth roughly $20 million, sources told HoopsHype. Sharpe has impressed Brooklyn and is expected to have a bigger role this season. Agents Aaron Reilly and Reggie Berry of AMR Agency finalized the deal.

items.title: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets plan to sign Josh Minott to a two-year, $9 million deal. Team option Year 2. He averaged 10.8 points on 49% shooting from the field and 40% from 3-point range in 19.3 minutes for Brooklyn last season. Agent Aaron Turner and Nets officials did the deal.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets plan to sign Josh Minott to a two-year, $9 million deal. Team option Year 2. He averaged 10.8 points on 49% shooting from the field and 40% from 3-point range in 19.3 minutes for Brooklyn last season. Agent Aaron Turner and Nets officials did the deal.

items.title: Nets could still have around $40M in cap space to use in free agency, pending how they time out acquiring Julius Randle.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Nets could still have around $40M in cap space to use in free agency, pending how they time out acquiring Julius Randle.

The signing of Day'Ron Sharpe will come after Nets have used their cap space, either through the Room Exception or remaining cap space, similiar to last year.

items.body_excerpt: I can confirm, via his lawyer Steve Haney, that Malik Beasley has been indicted on five counts including sports betting, money laundering, and wire fraud. They “look forward to defending all charges,” he says.

items.title: Brooklyn Nets forward Josh Minott intends to sign a new two-year, $9 million to return to the franchise, with a team option in second season, sources tell ESPN. The Nets declined Minott’s team option to clear the way for a new contract.

media.title: NBA vet Malik Beasley, former player Ed Davis indicted on illegal sports gambling charges (Gift Article)

items.title: Coming into 2025-26, no player in league history, over a full NBA season, had ever averaged:
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
Coming into 2025-26, no player in league history, over a full NBA season, had ever averaged:
more than 6.5 offensive rebounds and 
more than 2 blocks per-36 minutes. 

In 2025-26, 
Mitchell Robinson averaged:
 7.7 (!!) offensive rebounds and
2.1 blocks per-36 minutes
